title: Up to $250 Off WIRED’s Favorite Grills and Griddles for July 4
gist: A July 4 grill/griddle deals roundup where the reviewer's tested picks favor heat-distribution engineering, with the Recteq Flagship 1600 pellet smoker ($250 off) and Traeger Flatrock griddle as top recommendations.

insights.claim: The Recteq Flagship 1600 pellet smoker's centrally located firepot and symmetrical heat spreader produced only a 7-degree Fahrenheit difference from center to outside of the grill during low-and-slow cooks.
insights.speaker: Matthew Korfhage
insights.so_what: Even-heat engineering is the reviewer's primary quality signal, useful for judging which pellet-grill brands compete on performance versus price.
insights.quote: there was only a 7-degree Fahrenheit difference from the center to the outside.
insights.locator: The major selling point on this pellet smoker

insights.claim: The reviewer rates the three-burner Traeger Flatrock the best griddle tested in years, crediting its thermally conductive cold-rolled steel surface and U-shaped burner design for superior heat distribution.
insights.speaker: Matthew Korfhage
insights.so_what: Burner geometry and plate material, not price, drive griddle performance rankings, guiding value comparisons across the category.
insights.quote: It's the king of heat distribution, making seasoning easier and cooking more predictable.
insights.locator: The best griddle I've tested in the past couple years

insights.claim: The reviewer flags the $250 markdown on the Recteq Flagship 1600 (to $1,350) as the best deal in the roundup and the best price expected before end-of-summer closeouts, with the smoker carrying an MSRP of roughly a dollar per square inch across 1,600 square inches.
insights.speaker: Matthew Korfhage
insights.so_what: Timing guidance—best price before closeouts—signals promotional cadence for high-ticket outdoor cooking gear.
insights.quote: This $250-off deal matches the best I've seen this year, and the best I'd expect to see before end-of-summer closeouts.
insights.locator: The Flagship 1600 pellet smoker and grill

insights.claim: Because the Traeger Flatrock's $100 price cut is identical on both sizes, the two-burner version at $699 is the better outright value than the three-burner at $899.
insights.speaker: Matthew Korfhage
insights.so_what: Flat dollar discounts across SKUs make the cheaper unit the relatively better buy, a recurring value-shopping heuristic.
insights.quote: So the best deal outright is certainly on the two-burner version, down to just $699 for the 4th of July weekend.
insights.locator: This said, the $100 price cut is the same on both sizes.

insights.claim: Traeger's new lower-budget Irontop griddle line offers better wind shielding and leg stability and the same cook plate as the pricier Flatrock, but its simplified burner design creates noticeable hot spots toward the front, with a 36-inch 4-burner model at just $599.
insights.speaker: Matthew Korfhage
insights.so_what: The budget line trades even heating for a lower price, illustrating where manufacturers cut cost to hit aggressive price points.
insights.quote: the simplified burner design means there are much more noticeable hot spots, largely toward the front of the griddle above the burners.
insights.locator: Note that while there's no sale, Traeger released
